# Transcrição: 20. Middle Class Message 

- **Fonte:** 20. Middle Class Message 
- **Duração:** 02:25
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:25]

Let's face it, everybody's got two cents and everybody's got two minutes. So here's my two cents in under two minutes. Mr. Obama is in Kansas this past week and he did his speech called the make or break moment for the middle class. I'm like, dude, are you 50 years late in this program or what? A make or break moment? Do you really think there's a moment where somebody makes or breaks themselves and their finances? That's ridiculous. That's what he says. Everybody in this country succeeds when everyone gets a fair share. What do you mean? Everybody's not getting a fair share right now? I got my fair shot. Everybody that works here has their fair shot to improve their situation whenever they want. A guy's dumping trash, he gets his fair shot. He could own the company, manage the company, run the company. He could buy shares in the company. He could make friends with the rich guy he picks up the trash from. Where do you make your break? It's not a make or break moment for the middle class. He says, I believe this country succeeds when everyone gets a fair shot. What's a fair shot? A fair shot is waking up in the morning, going out into the marketplace, producing. He says, when everyone gets their fair share, what does that mean? Does that mean me and Fred should have the same share? We should just divide it up. There's a pie there. The hungry guy gets a bigger piece of the pie. Maybe he don't like cherries in his pie. And I love cherries. I got to have some cherries. I want to break that cherry up and eat it. So check it out. He says, when everyone gets their fair shot, when everyone gets their fair share, and when everyone plays by the same rules. Dude, I don't play by the same rules, Mr. Obama. I work 18 hours a day and another guy works eight. The rules are this, 24 hours in a day. You get to do what you want in America. It's free. It's a free country. We get to do what we want. You want to drive a cab or you want to own the cab company, okay? You want to run the business. You want to own the business. You want to work for the business. What place do you want to play at, man? Do you want to take the risk? Do you want to take the risk of the free enterprise? Put yourself at risk. Put your money, your finances, your actions, your name. What is all this about? Fair, man. Fair shot? I got a fair shot. You do too, okay? What about fair share? Hey, it's out there, man. Go get it. The middle class is suffering because they operated under just enough action, just enough savings, just enough investing, just enough courage. Get off the just enough program. Mr. Obama, you can't save the middle class. The middle class must save itself.
